content.full_text:
BRAD SHERMAN  
MEMBER  
STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION

March 18, 1991

Ms. Susan Bobrow, Esq.
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Bobrow:

Thank you for speaking to me on the telephone March 14, 1991. In our conversation, I inquired whether any provisions of the Political Reform Act were violated by an elected state officer's acceptance of a loan from a commercial lending institution in the amount of $35,000 at a rate of 11.5%, which loan was co-signed by another person. The loan was made directly to the elected state officer while he was a candidate and before he assumed his current elective state office, which is Member, Board of Equalization.

The loan was entered into April 15, 1990. I asked whether the 1990 cosigning, or any extension or renewal in 1991, would raise any issues under the Political Reform Act. You indicated that since there were no restrictions on gifts to elected state officers or candidates for such offices prior to January 1, 1991, the 1990 cosigning did not violate any provision of the Political Reform Act. However, you further indicated that any renewal or extension of the loan on or after January 1, 1991 by the cosigner would constitute a gift from the cosigner in the amount of any differential between fair market interest rates and the renewal interest rate. This gift would be subject to the $250.00 single-source gift limitation contained in Government Code section 89504, effective January 1, 1991.

Thank you for your prompt and informative response to my request for information. If you have any questions, please call.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Steven M. Kamp
Legal Counsel to Member

The image you've provided is mostly blank, with very faint and mostly illegible text in the upper right corner. The majority of the content cannot be reliably transcribed or summarized due to its extremely low quality and lack of clarity. If there is any specific detail or section you'd like me to focus on, please provide more context or another image.
